endpoints.description: Returns configuration information for a single port of this NodeBalancer.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{NODEBALANCERID}} with a real nodebalancerid. Replace {{CONFIGID}} with a real configid.

endpoints.name: Config Update
endpoints.description: Updates the configuration for a single port on a NodeBalancer.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{NODEBALANCERID}} with a real nodebalancerid. Replace {{CONFIGID}} with a real configid.

endpoints.name: Config Delete
endpoints.description:
Deletes the Config for a port of this NodeBalancer.

**This cannot be undone.**

Once completed, this NodeBalancer will no longer respond to requests on the given port. This also deletes all associated NodeBalancerNodes, but the Linodes they were routing traffic to will be unchanged and will not be removed.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{NODEBALANCERID}} with a real nodebalancerid. Replace {{CONFIGID}} with a real configid.

endpoints.name: Nodes List
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of NodeBalancer nodes associated with this Config. These are the backends that will be sent traffic for this port.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{NODEBALANCERID}} with a real nodebalancerid. Replace {{CONFIGID}} with a real configid.

endpoints.name: Node View
endpoints.description: Returns information about a single Node, a backend for this NodeBalancer's configured port.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{NODEBALANCERID}} with a real nodebalancerid. Replace {{NODEID}} with a real nodeid. Replace {{CONFIGID}} with a real configid.

endpoints.name: Node Update
endpoints.description: Updates information about a Node, a backend for this NodeBalancer's configured port.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{NODEBALANCERID}} with a real nodebalancerid. Replace {{NODEID}} with a real nodeid. Replace {{CONFIGID}} with a real configid.

endpoints.name: Node Delete
endpoints.description:
Deletes a Node from this Config. This backend will no longer receive traffic for the configured port of this NodeBalancer.

This does not change or remove the Linode whose address was used in the creation of this Node.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{NODEBALANCERID}} with a real nodebalancerid. Replace {{NODEID}} with a real nodeid. Replace {{CONFIGID}} with a real configid.

endpoints.name: NodeBalancer Statistics View
endpoints.description: Returns detailed statistics about the requested NodeBalancer.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{NODEBALANCERID}} with a real nodebalancerid.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Buckets List
endpoints.description:
Returns a paginated list of all Object Storage Buckets that you own.


This endpoint is available for convenience. It is recommended that instead you
use the more [fully-featured S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/serviceops/#list-buckets) directly.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Bucket Create
endpoints.description:
Creates an Object Storage Bucket in the specified cluster.

Accounts with negative balances cannot access this command.

If the bucket already exists and is owned by you, this endpoint returns a `200` response with that bucket as if it had just been created.

This endpoint is available for convenience. It is recommended that instead you use the more [fully-featured S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/bucketops/#put-bucket) directly.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Buckets in Cluster List
endpoints.description:
Returns a list of Buckets in this cluster belonging to this Account.


This endpoint is available for convenience. It is recommended that instead you
use the more [fully-featured S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/bucketops/#get-bucket) directly.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Bucket View
endpoints.description:
Returns a single Object Storage Bucket.


This endpoint is available for convenience. It is recommended that instead you
use the more [fully-featured S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/bucketops/#get-bucket) directly.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid. Replace {{BUCKET}} with a real bucket.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Bucket Remove
endpoints.description:
Removes a single bucket.

Bucket objects must be removed prior to removing the bucket. While buckets containing objects _may_ be
deleted using the [s3cmd command-line tool](/docs/products/storage/object-storage/guides/s3cmd/#delete-a-bucket), such operations
can fail if the bucket contains too many objects. The recommended
way to empty large buckets is to use the [S3 API to configure lifecycle policies](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/bucketpolicy/#) that
remove all objects, then delete the bucket.

This endpoint is available for convenience. It is recommended that instead you
use the more [fully-featured S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/bucketops/#delete-bucket) directly.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid. Replace {{BUCKET}} with a real bucket.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Bucket Access Modify
endpoints.description:
Allows changing basic Cross-origin Resource Sharing (CORS) and Access Control Level (ACL) settings.
Only allows enabling/disabling CORS for all origins, and/or setting canned ACLs.


For more fine-grained control of both systems, please use the more [fully-featured S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/bucketops/#put-bucket-acl) directly.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid. Replace {{BUCKET}} with a real bucket.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Bucket Access Update
endpoints.description:
Allows changing basic Cross-origin Resource Sharing (CORS) and Access Control Level (ACL) settings.
Only allows enabling/disabling CORS for all origins, and/or setting canned ACLs.


For more fine-grained control of both systems, please use the more [fully-featured S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/bucketops/#put-bucket-acl) directly.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid. Replace {{BUCKET}} with a real bucket.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Object ACL Config View
endpoints.description:
View an Object's configured Access Control List (ACL) in this Object Storage bucket.
ACLs define who can access your buckets and objects and specify the level of access
granted to those users.


This endpoint is available for convenience. It is recommended that instead you
use the more [fully-featured S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/objectops/#get-object-acl) directly.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid. Replace {{BUCKET}} with a real bucket.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Object ACL Config Update
endpoints.description:
Update an Object's configured Access Control List (ACL) in this Object Storage bucket.
ACLs define who can access your buckets and objects and specify the level of access
granted to those users.


This endpoint is available for convenience. It is recommended that instead you
use the more [fully-featured S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/objectops/#set-object-acl) directly.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid. Replace {{BUCKET}} with a real bucket.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Bucket Contents List
endpoints.description:
Returns the contents of a bucket. The contents are paginated using a `marker`,
which is the name of the last object on the previous page.  Objects may
be filtered by `prefix` and `delimiter` as well; see Query Parameters for more
information.


This endpoint is available for convenience. It is recommended that instead you
use the more [fully-featured S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/objectops/#get-object) directly.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid. Replace {{BUCKET}} with a real bucket.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Object URL Create
endpoints.description:
Creates a pre-signed URL to access a single Object in a bucket. This
can be used to share objects, and also to create/delete objects by using
the appropriate HTTP method in your request body's `method` parameter.


This endpoint is available for convenience. It is recommended that instead you
use the more [fully-featured S3 API](https://docs.ceph.com/en/latest/radosgw/s3/)
directly.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid. Replace {{BUCKET}} with a real bucket.

endpoints.name: Object Storage TLS/SSL Cert View
endpoints.description:
Returns a boolean value indicating if this bucket has a corresponding TLS/SSL certificate that was
uploaded by an Account user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid. Replace {{BUCKET}} with a real bucket.

endpoints.name: Object Storage TLS/SSL Cert Upload
endpoints.description:
Upload a TLS/SSL certificate and private key to be served when you visit your Object Storage bucket via HTTPS.
Your TLS/SSL certificate and private key are stored encrypted at rest.


To replace an expired certificate, [delete your current certificate](/docs/api/object-storage/#object-storage-tlsssl-cert-delete)
and upload a new one.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid. Replace {{BUCKET}} with a real bucket.

endpoints.name: Object Storage TLS/SSL Cert Delete
endpoints.description: Deletes this Object Storage bucket's user uploaded TLS/SSL certificate and private key.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid. Replace {{BUCKET}} with a real bucket.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Cancel
endpoints.description:
Cancel Object Storage on an Account.

**Warning**: Removes all buckets and their contents from your Account. This data is irretrievable once removed.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Clusters List
endpoints.description:
Returns a paginated list of Object Storage Clusters that are available for
use.  Users can connect to the clusters with third party clients to create buckets
and upload objects.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Cluster View
endpoints.description: Returns a single Object Storage Cluster.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{CLUSTERID}} with a real clusterid.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Keys List
endpoints.description:
Returns a paginated list of Object Storage Keys for authenticating to
the Object Storage S3 API.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Key View
endpoints.description: Returns a single Object Storage Key provisioned for your account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{KEYID}} with a real keyid.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Key Update
endpoints.description: Updates an Object Storage Key on your account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{KEYID}} with a real keyid.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Key Revoke
endpoints.description: Revokes an Object Storage Key.  This keypair will no longer be usable by third-party clients.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{KEYID}} with a real keyid.

endpoints.name: Object Storage Transfer View
endpoints.description:
The amount of outbound data transfer used by your account's Object Storage buckets.
Object Storage adds 1 terabyte of outbound data transfer to your data transfer pool.
See the [Object Storage Overview](/docs/products/storage/object-storage/#pricing)
guide for details on Object Storage transfer quotas.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Profile View
endpoints.description:
Returns information about the current User. This can be used to see who is acting in applications where more than one token is managed. For example, in third-party OAuth applications.

This endpoint is always accessible, no matter what OAuth scopes the acting token has.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Profile Update
endpoints.description: Update information in your Profile.  This endpoint requires the "account:read_write" OAuth Scope.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Authorized Apps List
endpoints.description: This is a collection of OAuth apps that you've given access to your Account, and includes the level of access granted.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Authorized App View
endpoints.description: Returns information about a single app you've authorized to access your Account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{APPID}} with a real appid.

endpoints.name: App Access Revoke
endpoints.description: Expires this app token. This token may no longer be used to access your Account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{APPID}} with a real appid.

endpoints.name: Trusted Devices List
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of active TrustedDevices for your User. Browsers with an active Remember Me Session are logged into your account until the session expires or is revoked.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Trusted Device View
endpoints.description: Returns a single active TrustedDevice for your User.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{DEVICEID}} with a real deviceid.

endpoints.name: Trusted Device Revoke
endpoints.description: Revoke an active TrustedDevice for your User.  Once a TrustedDevice is revoked, this device will have to log in again before accessing your Linode account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{DEVICEID}} with a real deviceid.

endpoints.name: Grants List
endpoints.description:
This returns a GrantsResponse describing what the acting User has been granted access to.  For unrestricted users, this will return a  204 and no body because unrestricted users have access to everything without grants.  This will not return information about entities you do not have access to.  This endpoint is useful when writing third-party OAuth applications to see what options you should present to the acting User.

For example, if they do not have `global.add_linodes`, you might not display a button to deploy a new Linode.

Any client may access this endpoint; no OAuth scopes are required.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Logins List
endpoints.description: Returns a collection of successful account logins from this user during the last 90 days.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Login View
endpoints.description: Returns a login object displaying information about a successful account login from this user.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{LOGINID}} with a real loginid.

endpoints.name: Phone Number Verification Code Send
endpoints.description:
Send a one-time verification code via SMS message to the submitted phone number. Providing your phone number helps ensure you can securely access your Account in case other ways to connect are lost. Your phone number is only used to verify your identity by sending an SMS message. Standard carrier messaging fees may apply.

* By accessing this command you are opting in to receive SMS messages. You can opt out of SMS messages by using the **Phone Number Delete** ([DELETE /profile/phone-number](/docs/api/profile/#phone-number-delete)) command after your phone number is verified.

* Verification codes are valid for 10 minutes after they are sent.

* Subsequent requests made prior to code expiration result in sending the same code.

Once a verification code is received, verify your phone number with the **Phone Number Verify** ([POST /profile/phone-number/verify](/docs/api/profile/#phone-number-verify)) command.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Phone Number Delete
endpoints.description:
Delete the verified phone number for the User making this request.

Use this command to opt out of SMS messages for the requesting User after a phone number has been verified with the **Phone Number Verify** ([POST /profile/phone-number/verify](/docs/api/profile/#phone-number-verify)) command.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Phone Number Verify
endpoints.description:
Verify a phone number by confirming the one-time code received via SMS message after accessing the **Phone Verification Code Send** ([POST /profile/phone-number](/docs/api/profile/#phone-number-verification-code-send)) command.

* Verification codes are valid for 10 minutes after they are sent.

* Only the same User that made the verification code request can use that code with this command.

Once completed, the verified phone number is assigned to the User making the request. To change the verified phone number for a User, first use the **Phone Number Delete** ([DELETE /profile/phone-number](/docs/api/profile/#phone-number-delete)) command, then begin the verification process again with the **Phone Verification Code Send** ([POST /profile/phone-number](/docs/api/profile/#phone-number-verification-code-send)) command.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: User Preferences View
endpoints.description:
View a list of user preferences tied to the OAuth client that generated
the token making the request. The user preferences endpoints allow
consumers of the API to store arbitrary JSON data, such as a user's font
size preference or preferred display name. User preferences are available
for each OAuth client registered to your account, and as such an account can
have multiple user preferences.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: User Preferences Update
endpoints.description: Updates a user's preferences. These preferences are tied to the OAuth client that generated the token making the request. The user preferences endpoints allow consumers of the API to store arbitrary JSON data, such as a user's font size preference or preferred display name. An account may have multiple preferences. Preferences, and the pertaining request body, may contain any arbitrary JSON data that the user would like to store.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Security Questions List
endpoints.description: Returns a collection of security questions and their responses, if any, for your User Profile.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Security Questions Answer
endpoints.description:
Adds security question responses for your User.

Requires exactly three unique questions.

Previous responses are overwritten if answered or reset to `null` if unanswered.

**Note**: Security questions must be answered for your User prior to accessing the **Two Factor Secret Create** ([POST /profile/tfa-enable](/docs/api/profile/#two-factor-secret-create)) command.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: SSH Keys List
endpoints.description: Returns a collection of SSH Keys you've added to your Profile.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: SSH Key Add
endpoints.description: Adds an SSH Key to your Account profile.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: SSH Key View
endpoints.description: Returns a single SSH Key object identified by `id` that you have access to view.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{SSHKEYID}} with a real sshkeyid.

endpoints.name: SSH Key Update
endpoints.description:
Updates an SSH Key that you have permission to `read_write`.

Only SSH key labels can be updated.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{SSHKEYID}} with a real sshkeyid.

endpoints.name: SSH Key Delete
endpoints.description:
Deletes an SSH Key you have access to.

**Note:** deleting an SSH Key will *not* remove it from any Linode or Disk that was deployed with `authorized_keys`. In those cases, the keys must be manually deleted on the Linode or Disk. This endpoint will only delete the key's association from your Profile.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{SSHKEYID}} with a real sshkeyid.

endpoints.name: Two Factor Authentication Disable
endpoints.description: Disables Two Factor Authentication for your User. Once successful, login attempts from untrusted computers will only require a password before being successful. This is less secure, and is discouraged.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Two Factor Secret Create
endpoints.description:
Generates a Two Factor secret for your User. To enable TFA for your User, enter the secret obtained from this command with the **Two Factor Authentication Confirm/Enable** ([POST /profile/tfa-enable-confirm](/docs/api/profile/#two-factor-authentication-confirmenable)) command.
Once enabled, logins from untrusted computers are required to provide
a TFA code before they are successful.

**Note**: Before you can enable TFA, security questions must be answered for your User by accessing the **Security Questions Answer** ([POST /profile/security-questions](/docs/api/profile/#security-questions-answer)) command.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Two Factor Authentication Confirm/Enable
endpoints.description: Confirms that you can successfully generate Two Factor codes and enables TFA on your Account. Once this is complete, login attempts from untrusted computers will be required to provide a Two Factor code before they are successful.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Personal Access Tokens List
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of Personal Access Tokens currently active for your User.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Personal Access Token Create
endpoints.description: Creates a Personal Access Token for your User. The raw token will be returned in the response, but will never be returned again afterward so be sure to take note of it. You may create a token with _at most_ the scopes of your current token. The created token will be able to access your Account until the given expiry, or until it is revoked.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Personal Access Token View
endpoints.description: Returns a single Personal Access Token.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{TOKENID}} with a real tokenid.

endpoints.name: Personal Access Token Update
endpoints.description: Updates a Personal Access Token.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{TOKENID}} with a real tokenid.

endpoints.name: Personal Access Token Revoke
endpoints.description: Revokes a Personal Access Token. The token will be invalidated immediately, and requests using that token will fail with a 401. It is possible to revoke access to the token making the request to revoke a token, but keep in mind that doing so could lose you access to the api and require you to create a new token through some other means.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{TOKENID}} with a real tokenid.

endpoints.name: Regions List
endpoints.description:
Lists the Regions available for Linode services. Not all services are guaranteed to be
available in all Regions.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Region View
endpoints.description: Returns a single Region.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{REGIONID}} with a real regionid.

endpoints.name: Support Tickets List
endpoints.description:
Returns a collection of Support Tickets on your Account. Support Tickets can be both tickets you open with Linode for support, as well as tickets generated by Linode regarding your Account.
This collection includes all Support Tickets generated on your Account, with open tickets returned first.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Support Ticket Open
endpoints.description:
Open a Support Ticket.
Only one of the ID attributes (`linode_id`, `domain_id`, etc.) can be set on a single Support Ticket.

body.summary: Having trouble resetting root password on my Linode
body.description: I'm having trouble setting the root password on my Linode. I tried following the instructions but something is not working and I'm not sure what I'm doing wrong. Can you please help me figure out how I can reset it?

endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Support Ticket View
endpoints.description: Returns a Support Ticket under your Account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{TICKETID}} with a real ticketid.

endpoints.name: Support Ticket Attachment Create
endpoints.description:
Adds a file attachment to an existing Support
Ticket on your Account. File attachments are used to assist our
Support team in resolving your Ticket. Examples of attachments
are screen shots and text files that provide additional information.

The file attachment is submitted in the request as multipart/form-data.

**Note**: Accepted file extensions include: .gif, .jpg, .jpeg, .pjpg,
.pjpeg, .tif, .tiff, .png, .pdf, or .txt.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{TICKETID}} with a real ticketid.

endpoints.name: Support Ticket Close
endpoints.description: Closes a Support Ticket you have access to modify.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{TICKETID}} with a real ticketid.

endpoints.name: Replies List
endpoints.description: Returns a collection of replies to a Support Ticket on your Account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{TICKETID}} with a real ticketid.

endpoints.name: Reply Create
endpoints.description: Adds a reply to an existing Support Ticket.

body.description: Thank you for your help. I was able to figure out what the problem was and I successfully reset my password. You guys are the best!

endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{TICKETID}} with a real ticketid.

endpoints.name: Tags List
endpoints.description:
Tags are User-defined labels attached to objects in your Account, such as Linodes. They are used for specifying and grouping attributes of objects that are relevant to the User.

This endpoint returns a paginated list of Tags on your account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: New Tag Create
endpoints.description:
Creates a new Tag and optionally tags requested objects with it immediately.

**Important**: You must be an unrestricted User in order to add or modify Tags.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Tagged Objects List
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of all objects you've tagged with the requested Tag. This is a mixed collection of all object types.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{LABEL}} with a real label.

endpoints.name: Tag Delete
endpoints.description:
Remove a Tag from all objects and delete it.

**Important**: You must be an unrestricted User in order to add or modify Tags.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{LABEL}} with a real label.

endpoints.name: Volumes List
endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of Volumes you have permission to view.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Volume Create
endpoints.description: Creates a Volume on your Account. In order for this to complete successfully, your User must have the `add_volumes` grant. Creating a new Volume will start accruing additional charges on your account.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY.

endpoints.name: Volume View
endpoints.description: Get information about a single Volume.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{VOLUMEID}} with a real volumeid.

endpoints.name: Volume Delete
endpoints.description:
Deletes a Volume you have permission to `read_write`.

* **Deleting a Volume is a destructive action and cannot be undone.**

* Deleting stops billing for the Volume. You will be billed for time used within
the billing period the Volume was active.

* Volumes that are migrating cannot be deleted until the migration is finished.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{VOLUMEID}} with a real volumeid.

endpoints.name: Volume Attach
endpoints.description: Attaches a Volume on your Account to an existing Linode on your Account. In order for this request to complete successfully, your User must have `read_only` or `read_write` permission to the Volume and `read_write` permission to the Linode. Additionally, the Volume and Linode must be located in the same Region.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{VOLUMEID}} with a real volumeid.

endpoints.name: Volume Clone
endpoints.description:
Creates a Volume on your Account. In order for this request to complete successfully, your User must have the `add_volumes` grant. The new Volume will have the same size and data as the source Volume. Creating a new Volume will incur a charge on your Account.
* Only Volumes with a `status` of "active" can be cloned.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{VOLUMEID}} with a real volumeid.

endpoints.name: Volume Detach
endpoints.description: Detaches a Volume on your Account from a Linode on your Account. In order for this request to complete successfully, your User must have `read_write` access to the Volume and `read_write` access to the Linode.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{VOLUMEID}} with a real volumeid.

endpoints.name: Volume Resize
endpoints.description:
Resize an existing Volume on your Account. In order for this request to complete successfully, your User must have the `read_write` permissions to the Volume.
* Volumes can only be resized up.
* Only Volumes with a `status` of "active" can be resized.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{LINODE_API_KEY}} with your LINODE API KEY. Replace {{VOLUMEID}} with a real volumeid.
